section_miscategorizations.text: He states his father had kidney stones and he believes he has passed a couple in the past. He has never had a kidney stone before.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These two consecutive sentences conflict (having passed stones vs. never having had one). While both belong in Subjective, the internal inconsistency could confuse interpretation of the history, but it is not a section-placement error.

section_miscategorizations.text: A stone measuring 5 cm is noted in the proximal right ureter on CT imaging.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The content is correctly in Objective as imaging, but the size (5 cm) is implausibly large for a ureteral stone and could mislead clinically; however, this is a content accuracy issue, not a section miscategorization.
